name: Execution Depth Dominance
definition: Execution Depth Dominance explains when field-bound review succeeds or fails under external evidence.
mechanism: Claim decisions depend on which evidence fields are specific, actionable, replayed, and practical, not only on field count.
nonExamples: source URL with no execution evidence
predictions: specific missing execution fields will downgrade claims
burdensome rules will overblock some practical claims
falsifiers: missing fields do not change claim safety
